query: What is the concept of genealogy according to Deleuze's reading of Nietzsche?
expected_quotes: Nietzsche creates the new concept of genealogy
the value of origin and the origin of values
the differential element of values from which their value itself derives
label: np_genealogy_broad

query: How does Deleuze distinguish active and reactive forces in Nietzsche and Philosophy?
expected_quotes: A force is domination, but also the object on which domination is exercised
The being of force is plural
Pluralism is the properly philosophical way of thinking
label: np_active_reactive_specific

query: What is the role of the will to power in Nietzsche's philosophy as interpreted by Deleuze?
expected_quotes: the will is not exercised mysteriously on muscles or nerves
the real problem is not that of the relation of will to the involuntary
Their knowing is creating, their creating is a law-giving
label: np_will_to_power_broad

query: What are the different types of signs in Proust's work according to Deleuze?
expected_quotes: Learning is essentially concerned with signs
signs are the object of a temporal apprenticeship
all the signs converge upon art
label: ps_types_signs_broad

query: How does Deleuze connect involuntary memory to art in Proust and Signs?
expected_quotes: all apprenticeships are already unconscious apprenticeships to art itself
an essence is incarnated in involuntary memory
the essential is in the signs of art
label: ps_memory_art_specific

query: How does Deleuze describe the role of disappointment in learning in Proust and Signs?
expected_quotes: Disappointment is a fundamental moment of search or of apprenticeship
the first time is the time of inexperience
the object interposes and confuses the signs
label: ps_disappointment_niche

query: What is Bergson's method of intuition as described by Deleuze?
expected_quotes: perception is not the object plus something but the object minus something
conditions of real experience
To open us up to the inhuman and the superhuman
label: berg_intuition_method_broad

query: How does Deleuze explain the contemporaneity of past and present in Bergsonism?
expected_quotes: How would a new present come about if the old present did not pass
The past would never be constituted if it had not been constituted first of all
the present itself is only the most contracted level of the past
label: berg_past_present_specific

query: What is the relationship between repetition and difference according to Deleuze?
expected_quotes: Repetition is not generality
If exchange is the criterion of generality, theft and gift are those of repetition
The modern world is one of simulacra
label: dr_repetition_difference_broad

query: How does Deleuze critique the philosophy of representation in Difference and Repetition?
expected_quotes: The primacy of identity defines the world of representation
difference and repetition have taken the place of the identical and the negative
Problems-Ideas are positive multiplicities
label: dr_critique_representation_specific

query: What is the concept of individuation in Difference and Repetition?
expected_quotes: Individuation as such operates beneath all forms
inseparable from a pure ground that it brings to the surface
fields of fluid intensive factors
label: dr_individuation_niche

query: What is the Stoic theory of sense and events as presented in The Logic of Sense?
source: 1969 The Logic of Sense - Deleuze, Gilles.pdf
expected_quotes: They are not things or facts, but events
causes of certain things of an entirely different nature
the paradox of infinite identity
label: ls_stoic_events_broad

query: How does Deleuze contrast Carroll and Artaud in The Logic of Sense?
source: 1969 The Logic of Sense - Deleuze, Gilles.pdf
expected_quotes: I have not produced a translation of Jabberwocky
the distance separating Carroll and Artaud
an affected infantilism
label: ls_carroll_artaud_specific

query: How does Deleuze distinguish ethics from morality in Spinoza: Practical Philosophy?
expected_quotes: Law does not provide us with any knowledge
the immanent power that determines the qualitative difference of modes of existence
In a world consumed by the negative
label: sp_ethics_morality_broad

query: What is the role of affects in Spinoza's philosophy according to Deleuze?
expected_quotes: the passage to a greater perfection is called joy
the power of acting varies according to external causes
a diminution of the power of acting is called sadness
label: sp_affects_specific

query: How does Deleuze characterize Spinoza's critique of sad passions?
expected_quotes: The slave, the tyrant, and the priest
the moralist trinity
sad spirits need a tyrant in order to be content
label: sp_sad_passions_niche

query: What is the concept of desiring-production in Anti-Oedipus?
source: 1972 Anti-Oedipus Capitalism and Schizophr - Deleuze, Gilles.pdf
expected_quotes: It is at work everywhere, functioning smoothly at times
Everywhere it is machines-real ones, not figurative ones
a process that produces the one within the other
label: ao_desiring_production_broad

query: How do Deleuze and Guattari critique Oedipus in Anti-Oedipus?
source: 1972 Anti-Oedipus Capitalism and Schizophr - Deleuze, Gilles.pdf
expected_quotes: the domain of free syntheses where everything is possible
the whole of desiring-production is crushed
the reproduction of desire gives way to a simple representation
label: ao_critique_oedipus_specific

query: How do Deleuze and Guattari describe the body without organs in Anti-Oedipus?
source: 1972 Anti-Oedipus Capitalism and Schizophr - Deleuze, Gilles.pdf
expected_quotes: a pure fluid in a free state
the body without organs exerts later on in the uninterrupted process
the body without organs that has become a dead thing
label: ao_bwo_niche

query: What is the concept of lines in Deleuze's Dialogues?
expected_quotes: we are made up of lines and these lines are very varied in nature
lines of flight, where the fluxes are combined
family-profession; job-holiday
label: dial_lines_broad

query: How does Deleuze describe the history of philosophy as a form of repression in Dialogues?
expected_quotes: A formidable school of intimidation
The exercise of thought thus conforms to the goals of the real State
Sartre was our Outside
label: dial_history_philosophy_specific

query: What does Deleuze mean by becoming in Dialogues?
expected_quotes: writing always encounters a minority which does not write
animal-becomings in literature which do not consist in talking of one's dog or cat
an encounter between two reigns, a short-circuit
label: dial_becoming_niche

query: What is the rhizome concept as introduced in A Thousand Plateaus?
expected_quotes: A book has neither object nor subject
lines of articulation or segmentarity, strata and territories
There is no difference between what a book talks about and how it is made
label: atp_rhizome_broad

query: What is the Body without Organs in A Thousand Plateaus?
expected_quotes: You never reach the Body without Organs
you are forever attaining it, it is a limit
there is nothing more useless than an organ
label: atp_bwo_broad

query: How do Deleuze and Guattari define the war machine?
expected_quotes: The war machine is exterior to the State apparatus
two heads: the magician-king and the jurist-priest
A State becomes totalitarian
label: atp_war_machine_specific

query: What is micropolitics according to Deleuze and Guattari in A Thousand Plateaus?
expected_quotes: A State becomes totalitarian
simultaneous states of the abstract Machine
the three lines do not only coexist but transform themselves into one another
label: atp_micropolitics_niche

query: What is the concept of sensation in Francis Bacon: The Logic of Sensation?
source: 1981 Francis Bacon The Logic of Sensation - Deleuze, Gilles.pdf
expected_quotes: one discovers rhythm as the essence of painting
Painting has to extract the Figure from the figurative
The entire body escapes through the screaming mouth
label: fb_sensation_broad

query: What is the role of the diagram in Bacon's painting according to Deleuze?
source: 1981 Francis Bacon The Logic of Sensation - Deleuze, Gilles.pdf
expected_quotes: the operative set of asignifying and nonrepresentative lines and zones
its function is to be suggestive
introduce possibilities of fact
label: fb_diagram_specific

query: What is Bergson's first thesis on movement according to Cinema 1?
expected_quotes: Cinema does not give us an image to which movement is added
it immediately gives us a movement-image
relating movement not to privileged instants but to any-instant-whatever
label: c1_bergson_theses_broad

query: How does Deleuze distinguish ancient and modern conceptions of movement in Cinema 1?
expected_quotes: movement refers to intelligible elements, Forms or Ideas
the modern scientific revolution has consisted in relating movement
from formal transcendental elements to immanent material elements
label: c1_ancient_modern_movement_specific

query: What is the concept of the new archivist in Deleuze's Foucault?
expected_quotes: A new archivist has been appointed
he will deal only with statements
skimming along in a kind of diagonal line
label: fouc_archivist_broad

query: How does Deleuze characterize the relationship between seeing and speaking in Foucault?
expected_quotes: There is a disjunction between speaking and seeing
what we see never lies in what we say
Truth is defined neither by conformity nor common form
label: fouc_seeing_speaking_specific

query: What does the fold signify in Deleuze's reading of Leibniz?
source: 1988 The Fold Leibniz and the Baroque - Deleuze, Gilles.pdf
expected_quotes: The Baroque refers not to an essence but rather to an operative function
the Baroque fold unfurls all the way to infinity
The condition of closure holds for the infinite opening of the finite
label: fold_concept_broad

query: How does Deleuze connect Leibniz's monads to the concept of expression in The Fold?
source: 1988 The Fold Leibniz and the Baroque - Deleuze, Gilles.pdf
expected_quotes: the soul is the expression of the world
the world must be placed in the subject
This is the torsion that constitutes the fold of the world and of the soul
label: fold_monad_expression_specific

query: What is the pure optical situation in Cinema 2?
expected_quotes: What defines neo-realism is this build-up of purely optical situations
This is a cinema of the seer and no longer of the agent
He records rather than reacts
label: c2_optical_situation_broad

query: How does Deleuze characterize the crisis of the action-image in Cinema 2?
expected_quotes: the character has become a kind of viewer
the slackening of the sensory-motor connections
opsigns and sonsigns
label: c2_crisis_action_image_specific

query: What is the concept in philosophy according to What Is Philosophy?
expected_quotes: philosophy is the art of forming, inventing, and fabricating concepts
Concepts are not waiting for us ready-made
the concept has nothing whatever to do with a general or abstract idea
label: wip_concept_broad

query: What is the plane of immanence in What Is Philosophy?
expected_quotes: The plane of immanence has two facets as Thought and as Nature
thinking and being are said to be one and the same
a gigantic shuttle
label: wip_plane_immanence_specific

query: How do Deleuze and Guattari distinguish philosophy from science in What Is Philosophy?
expected_quotes: Chaos is defined not so much by its disorder as by the infinite speed
philosophy gives consistency to the virtual through concepts
science gives a reference to the virtual which actualizes it through functions
label: wip_philosophy_science_broad

query: How does the concept of becoming differ between The Logic of Sense and A Thousand Plateaus?
source: 1969 The Logic of Sense - Deleuze, Gilles.pdf
expected_quotes: the paradox of this pure becoming with its capacity to elude the present
the younger becoming older than the older
You never reach the Body without Organs
label: cross_becoming_ls_atp_comparative

query: How does Deleuze's treatment of forces connect Nietzsche and Philosophy to Spinoza: Practical Philosophy?
expected_quotes: The being of force is plural
the passage to a greater perfection is called joy
confidence in life, in the power of life
label: cross_forces_np_sp_comparative

query: How does the concept of the diagram function across Foucault and Francis Bacon?
expected_quotes: the operative set of asignifying and nonrepresentative lines
A new archivist has been appointed
confinement a secondary element
label: cross_diagram_fouc_fb_comparative

query: How does Deleuze's concept of the virtual evolve from Bergsonism to Difference and Repetition?
expected_quotes: The possible is a false notion
it is not the real that resembles the possible
All identities are only simulated
label: cross_virtual_berg_dr_comparative

query: How does the concept of the movement-image in Cinema 1 relate to Bergsonism?
expected_quotes: Cinema does not give us an image to which movement is added
Cinema seems to be the last descendant of this lineage which Bergson traced
the discovery of the movement-image
label: cross_movement_c1_berg_comparative

query: How do Deleuze and Guattari's concepts of deterritorialization develop from Anti-Oedipus to A Thousand Plateaus?
expected_quotes: lines of flight, movements of deterritorialization and destratification
A State becomes totalitarian
The war machine is exterior to the State apparatus
label: cross_deterrit_ao_atp_comparative

query: What is the event according to Deleuze across The Logic of Sense and What Is Philosophy?
expected_quotes: The event is not the state of affairs
a virtual that is real without being actual, ideal without being abstract
They are not things or facts, but events
label: cross_event_ls_wip_comparative

query: How does Deleuze characterize Rossellini's films in Cinema 2?
expected_quotes: Rossellini's great quartet
Germany Year 0 presents a child who visits a foreign country
she sees, she has learnt to see
label: c2_rossellini_niche

query: What role does the scream play in Bacon's paintings according to Deleuze?
source: 1981 Francis Bacon The Logic of Sensation - Deleuze, Gilles.pdf
expected_quotes: The entire body escapes through the screaming mouth
beyond the scream there is the smile
the hysterical smile
label: fb_scream_niche

query: How does Deleuze explain Foucault's analysis of power as distinct from confinement?
expected_quotes: Foucault has always considered confinement a secondary element
the problem is not one of confinement but concerns the highways
gauging the degree to which the disciplinary diagram was fulfilled
label: fouc_power_confinement_niche

query: What is the significance of the Baroque fold for Deleuze's philosophy?
source: 1988 The Fold Leibniz and the Baroque - Deleuze, Gilles.pdf
expected_quotes: The Baroque refers not to an essence but rather to an operative function
the Baroque fold unfurls all the way to infinity
a flexible or an elastic body still has cohering parts
label: fold_baroque_significance_niche
